    Feed post

    Suggested

    Maggie Ma

    • 2nd

    Data Scientist | Tech Content Creator | AI Educator | 3x LinkedIn Learning Instructor

    Visit my website

    2d •

    My path to becoming a data scientist wasn’t linear. Before that role, I worked in several different positions: research, analysis, internships, and technical roles across different teams.

    At the time, it often felt like I was just trying to figure things out.

    But looking back, each experience added a piece to the puzzle:

    • learning how to analyze real-world data
    • turning insights into decisions
    • communicating technical ideas clearly
    • building systems that scale

    None of those roles felt like the “final destination.” But together, they shaped the skills I use today.

    Careers rarely follow a straight path and that’s often a good thing.

    Curious to hear from others here: What was a role in your career that unexpectedly helped you later on?

    #datascience #careersintech #womenintech #careergrowth #techcareers

    Every job I had before becoming a data scientist
